rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees with valid component scope and type alignment.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the nature of the statement.
Align helper declarations with actual expression types to avoid type mismatch errors.
Avoid referencing AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as typed variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid visible identifiers from the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type scopes.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing tokens
Implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
